title: Praxis Audiology (5343) — 200 Source Questions + 300 Guided Review
description: Contains 200 source diagnostic questions and 300 source-derived guided-review tasks; the assistant audit found 100 distinct source content kernels. Two independently authored 100-question diagnostic batches linked to a 12-chapter learning library, targeted skill practice, and an optional 120-question timed simulation. The official 5343 test currently has 120 selected-response questions in 120 minutes; AlloFlow practice is not an official form or score.
contentReview: Assistant audit completed: the 200 source questions contain 100 distinct content kernels and 100 parallel variants under the normalized answer-set/rationale/reference test. The 300 additional activities are assistant-reviewed guided reasoning transformations, not independent exam questions. The 500-distinct-question target is not met.
disclaimer: Independent preparation material. Not affiliated with or endorsed by ETS or ASHA. The official Praxis Audiology (5343) test currently has 120 selected-response questions in 120 minutes; the 100-item diagnostic batches are not official-length forms. Practice results are not official or scaled Praxis scores, pass predictions, credentials, clinical evaluations, diagnoses, medical or vestibular decisions, device fittings, treatment, legal advice, or substitutes for qualified supervision, urgent medical care, and current federal, state, local, licensure, certification, educational, health-care, privacy, consent, emergency, and reporting requirements.

items.prompt: What is the primary function of the ossicular chain?
choices: They transform acoustic energy and improve impedance matching between air and cochlear fluid.
They generate neural action potentials.
They analyze speech meaning.
They equalize endolymph and perilymph composition.
items.rationale: The middle-ear system transfers energy from air to fluid through area and lever relationships while preserving a usable acoustic signal.
choiceRationales: Correct. The middle-ear system transfers energy from air to fluid through area and lever relationships while preserving a usable acoustic signal.

items.prompt: How are frequency, wavelength, and sound speed related?
